question: What does React 19 automatically do with <title>, <meta>, and <link> tags in JSX?

options.text: Renders them at the location where they are declared in JSX
options.explanation: React 19 automatically finds these tags and moves them to <head>, regardless of where they are declared.

options.text: Converts them to document.title and meta API calls
options.explanation: React uses DOM operations to insert tags into <head>, not JavaScript APIs.

options.text: Ignores them and outputs a warning
options.explanation: React 19 specifically supports Document Metadata. Tags are processed and hoisted to <head>.

question: What is the precedence attribute used for in the <link rel="stylesheet"> tag?

options.text: To set the loading priority and style order
To specify the CSS file version
options.explanation: precedence controls the loading priority and order of style application, not versioning.
precedence determines the priority, not loading conditions. Styles are always loaded, but in a specific order.
Caching is managed by HTTP headers, not the precedence attribute.

question: Which Preload API function loads a resource AND executes it?

options.explanation: preload() only loads the resource into the browser cache but does not execute it.
prefetchDNS() only performs DNS resolution for a domain, without loading or executing the resource.
preconnect() establishes a connection (DNS + TCP + TLS) but does not load the resource.

question: Which preloading functions are available in React 19?

options.explanation: The prefetch() function does not exist in React 19 Preload API. Use preloadModule() for module preloading.

question: What advantage does Document Metadata in React 19 provide compared to react-helmet?

options.text: Metadata updates in real-time and requires no third-party libraries
Metadata is accessible to search crawlers without SSR
options.explanation: SSR or SSG is still needed for SEO. Client-side rendering of metadata does not help search crawlers.

options.text: Metadata is cached between page reloads
options.explanation: Metadata in React 19 lives in memory and is not cached between reloads.

options.text: You can use arbitrary HTML tags in <head>
options.explanation: React 19 supports specific tags: title, meta, link. Arbitrary tags are not processed.
